The '''''Guardian''-class light cruiser''', also known as '''''Guardian''-class Customs corvette''',<ref name="THG">''[[The Hutt Gambit]]''</ref> was an Inter-System Customs Vessel used primarily by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].

==Characteristics==
Fast, heavily-defended, and extremely well armed, the ''Guardian''-class was designed to patrol the remote and hostile systems at the edges of [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] space unescorted and for prolonged amounts of time.<ref name="TFUCG" /> More than capable of handling [[Freighter/Legends|freight]] traffic, [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]], and [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]], the ''Guardian'' was not well equipped for large-scale boarding actions as it lacked the space for a sizable boarding party, with crews often opting to simply destroy targets in true [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] fashion.<ref name="PP" /> To assist in interdiction and customs, ''Guardians'' could be outfitted with external docking points to carry [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE Fighters]]; however, these fighters reduced maneuverability noticeably.<ref name="SotSR" />

==History==
The ''Guardian''-class was one of the three main ship classes employed by [[Imperial Office of Customs/Legends|Imperial Customs]], and was a common Imperial system patrol craft.

''Guardian''s, which were in service from before the [[Battle of Nar Shaddaa (4 BBY)|Battle of Nar Shaddaa]],<ref name="THG" /> were a common sight in lightly populated, isolated systems where the Galactic Empire needed to maintain a presence without underwriting the expense of assigning a larger vessel to patrol duty. Also, these light cruisers often patrolled with corvette or frigate support.

These ships also saw use outside the Imperial military. The privately owned station known as "[[The Wheel/Legends|The Wheel]]," a gambling haven located in the [[Besh Gorgon system/Legends|Besh Gorgon System]] employed ten of these cruisers for traffic control duty in its security forces. Political entities such as the [[House Pelagia]] in the [[Tapani sector/Legends|Tapani sector]] maintained these ships as well. One such ship was the ''[[Reprise]]''.<ref name="Tapani Sector Instant Adventures" />

A variant of the Guardian was the [[ISB operations ship]], which was essentially the same ship modified to contain holding cells, as well as light and sensor stealth coatings.

The [[Guardian 334-class light cruiser|''Guardian 344''-class]] was later made by [[Sienar Fleet Systems/Legends|Sienar Fleet Systems]] to succeed the ''Guardian''-class. It addressed the previous cruiser's lack of space for a boarding party.<ref name="POTG,V3">''[[Planets of the Galaxy, Volume Three]]''</ref>

Pirates such as [[Shanto Dhil]] acquired ''Guardian''-class cruisers as well. Dhil's ''Guardian'' light cruiser, the ''[[Avatar's Needle]]'' liked to pose as an Imperial vessel in order to get close enough to bacta transports to steal their cargo.<ref name="LOTE">''[[Lords of the Expanse]]''</ref>
